old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-v2ct","title":"serial.py: _loop attribute access without null check causes AttributeError","description":"File: python/src/lichen/interface/kiss/serial.py
Lines: 93, 164

The send_frame() method uses self._loop.run_in_executor() but _loop can be None if open() was never called successfully:

Line 93:
```python
await self._loop.run_in_executor(None, _write)  # _loop could be None
```

While send_frame() checks _serial is not None, it does not check _loop. If open() failed partway (serial opened but _loop not set) or was never called, this raises AttributeError.

Similarly in close() at line 164:
```python
if self._loop:  # OK, checks first
    await self._loop.run_in_executor(None, _close)
```
But this only matters if _serial exists without _loop.

Impact: Unexpected AttributeError crash when calling send_frame on a connection that was not properly opened.

Fix: Add explicit check for _loop in send_frame():
```python
if self._closed or self._serial is None or self._loop is None:
    raise ConnectionError("serial port not open")
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:17:18.488431Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:17:18.488459Z"}

```","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:17:18.488431Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T22:24:09.306716448Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T22:24:09.306716448Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T22:29:09.306716448Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T22:24:09.306716448Z"}

new_value: Added _loop is None guard to send_frame() and recv() (consistent with existing patterns and close()). Removed dead comments. Prevents AttributeError on unopened connection. Commit 8ceec6c5b.
